one_liner: LLY surging 7% on EMA approval of Jaypirca and a BioArctic research collaboration — Wyckoff Phase C-D accumulation with 4/5 signals detected and strong DCF upside to $1,980.

news_catalyst.headline: Eli Lilly Wins EMA Backing For Jaypirca In Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia; BioArctic Collaboration Announced
news_catalyst.summary: Eli Lilly is up 7.1% today on two simultaneous catalysts: the EMA recommended approval of Jaypirca (pirtobrutinib) for chronic lymphocytic leukemia in Europe, and a new research collaboration agreement with BioArctic AB. The stock is breaking above its 90-day Wyckoff resistance at $1,170 on 2.35x average volume — a textbook high-volume accumulation bar confirming the Phase C-D transition. IBD notes LLY is leading a market rally rotation into defensive/healthcare names, reinforcing the institutional buying signal.

technical.summary: LLY is breaking out above its 90-day resistance of $1,170 on climactic accumulation volume (2.35x avg) with RSI at 72.7 — elevated but confirming trend strength rather than signaling reversal. MACD in bullish crossover. Four of five Wyckoff accumulation signals are detected (Phase C-D), suggesting the smart money positioning phase is near completion and markup is beginning. The $1,170 level, previously resistance, should now act as support.

valuation.summary: DCF base case of $1,980 implies 64% upside from current price, driven by 36% projected FCF growth from GLP-1/weight loss drug leadership. Even the bear case at $941 is only 22% below current price — asymmetric risk/reward.

key_strengths: Strong pipeline including oncology (Jaypirca, cancer assets)
key_concerns: High valuation relative to near-term earnings
Pipeline binary risk on clinical outcomes
fundamentals.summary: Grade B fundamentals with strong growth and health scores. The 36% DCF growth rate reflects genuine analyst consensus around Lilly's GLP-1 dominance and oncology pipeline — not speculative. Upcoming EMA approval of Jaypirca in CLL expands the revenue base beyond metabolic disease.

risks: RSI at 72.7 — overbought short-term; a 3-5% pullback to $1,170 support is likely before continuation
GLP-1 competitive pressure from Novo Nordisk semaglutide pipeline
Clinical trial failures could reverse the entire thesis quickly
